safetyChecks.evidence: All stepByStepActions and warnings cross-reference research packet findings. Key mappings: weir construction (FM 21-76 Chapter 8), gorge hook (Wilderness College), dawn/dusk timing (TakeMeFishing.org), 145°F cook temperature (FDA Hazards Guidance), Giardia inactivation (WMS Guidelines), bait sources (Wilderness College), field dressing (FM 21-76 Chapter 8), preservation (FM 21-76 Chapter 8)

No medical or safety claims appear beyond what is in the research packet. All 8 warnings and 8 whatNotToDo items trace back to research findings. The refraction correction tip (aim below apparent fish position) maps to the Wilderness College spearing guidance. The 200-foot innard disposal distance is a well-established Leave No Trace / camping hygiene standard consistent with FM 21-76 field dressing guidance.
8 warnings present covering food safety, legal status, energy expenditure, parasite risk, refraction, wildlife attraction, and preservation
5 redFlags present covering foodborne illness symptoms, prolonged failure to catch, algal bloom detection, dehydration priority, and abnormal fish appearance

Evidence tier is authoritative_plus_field_practice. sourceQuality='mixed' is appropriate: FM 21-76 (U.S. Army), FDA, and Wilderness Medical Society provide authoritative grounding; Wilderness College and Willow Haven Outdoor are field-practice sources. The mixed designation correctly reflects this split. No authoritative_only tier was required.
N/A — integrationDecision is 'new', not 'upgrade'; no previous guide to compare against

alternativeToGuideSlugs ['fishing-techniques', 'foraging', 'trapping'] are in parentTopic 'Water Collection and Basic Treatment Without Equipment', not in 'Emergency Food Procurement'. blockedByConstraints=['cant_move'] is logically consistent: primitive fishing requires scouting water bodies, building weirs, collecting bait, and moving to productive spots. A user who cannot move cannot perform any of these steps. The block is correct.

warnings: alt_slugs_same_topic: alternativeToGuideSlugs point to guides in a different parentTopic ('Water Collection and Basic Treatment Without Equipment') because 'Emergency Food Procurement' is a new parentTopic with no sibling guides yet; this will resolve naturally as additional food procurement guides are added to the cluster
recommendation: Guide passes all schema and safety checks with no blocking issues. One non-blocking warning exists about cross-parentTopic alternative slugs, which is expected for the first guide in a new parentTopic. Guide may proceed to Import/Staging. The human reviewer should note: (1) the 'Emergency Food Procurement' parentTopic will be auto-created on import, and (2) the alternative slug links to 'fishing-techniques', 'foraging', and 'trapping' provide useful cross-referencing even across parentTopics.
